expectations.text: Output identifies at least one duplicate object-type group using exact field/type matching criteria.
expectations.evidence: Mentions duplicate groups and exact matching criteria.

expectations.text: Output explicitly states that non-breaking behavior is the default and that breaking merges require opt-in.
expectations.evidence: States non-breaking default and requires explicit/opt-in for breaking migration.

expectations.text: Output includes a per-group disposition (merged, implementation-only, or skipped) with rationale.
expectations.evidence: Provides per-group action/disposition and accompanying reason.

expectations.text: Output treats input object types as a separate duplicate-detection category from object types.
expectations.evidence: Focuses duplicate detection on input object types.

expectations.text: Output avoids cross-kind merges (object to input or input to object).
expectations.evidence: Not clearly evidenced in output: Output avoids cross-kind merges (object to input or input to object).

expectations.text: Output flags any public-contract-breaking merge candidates as skipped pending explicit approval.
expectations.evidence: Marks duplicates as skipped due to breaking/public API risk.

expectations.text: Output requires exact-shape equality and does not merge near duplicates.

expectations.text: Output explains that directive/default-value mismatches are excluded from automatic consolidation by default.

notes: The with_skill configuration outperformed without_skill by 0.33 pass rate on average (0.89 vs 0.5567).
Across both configurations, eval 2 was the recurring weakness: the output did not clearly evidence avoidance of cross-kind merges between object and input types.
With the skill, eval 1 and eval 3 met all three expectations, including exact-shape matching, non-breaking-by-default behavior, and clear per-group dispositions.
Without the skill, eval 1 missed the non-breaking-default and per-group-disposition expectations, and eval 3 missed the exact-shape-equality expectation.
